stories.headline: Spain closes airspace to US aircraft involved in Iran war: It follows a decision by the Spanish government to deny the US use of the two jointly run military bases in Andalusia.
stories.summary: Spain has closed its airspace to US aircraft involved in the Iran war, denying the US access to two jointly run military bases in Andalusia.

stories.headline: Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks face death penalty under new Israeli law: The new law, passed on Monday, was pushed hard by the far-right and Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.
stories.summary: A new Israeli law, strongly advocated by far-right figures, now allows for the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks.

stories.headline: Most Syrian refugees in Germany expected to return home in three years, Merz says: Merz’s government has taken a tougher line on refugees and migrants, amid surging support for the anti-immigration AfD party.
stories.summary: Germany's government, led by Merz, anticipates most Syrian refugees will return home within three years, reflecting a tougher stance on immigration amidst rising support for the anti-immigration AfD party.

stories.headline:
News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar:

Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’
stories.summary: Several news outlets incorrectly reported that Somaliland requested the extradition of Ilhan Omar, with these reports stemming from an unofficial X post.

stories.headline:
Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia:

Abnormally strong jet stream triggers deluge in Middle East, while north Africa braces for 60-80mph gusts

An unusual weather pattern unleashed severe thunderstorms across parts of the Middle
stories.summary: Severe thunderstorms, triggered by an unusually strong jet stream, have brought heavy deluges to parts of the UAE and Saudi Arabia.

stories.headline:
At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack:

Nearly 6,000 people forced to flee, human rights group says, as it criticises ‘abandonment’ from authorities

At least 70 people have been killed and 30 injured during an attack in Haiti’s bre
stories.summary: A gang attack in Haiti has resulted in at least 70 deaths and 30 injuries, displacing nearly 6,000 people, with human rights groups criticizing authorities for their inaction.

stories.headline: Elon Musk in a post on X says some of his Trump posts ‘went too far’ and that he regrets them:
stories.summary: Elon Musk stated on X that he regrets some of his posts about Trump, admitting they

stories.headline: Swiss National Bank cuts interest rates by a half point to 0.5%:
stories.summary: The Swiss National Bank has reduced its interest rates by half a point, bringing them down to 0.5%.

stories.headline: Fed’s Powell says rate cut ‘on the table’ as soon as September:
stories.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell indicated that a potential interest rate cut could be considered as early as September.

stories.headline: Artists left devastated after floods destroy renowned NT arts centre
stories.summary: The Merrepen Arts Centre in Nauiyu, a remote Northern Territory community, has been destroyed by recent flooding, leaving local artists facing significant loss.

stories.headline: Melbourne childcare centre suspended for 90 days following multiple breaches
stories.summary: Milestones Early Learning Werribee has had its operations suspended by Victoria's new childcare watchdog due to multiple serious and concerning issues identified at the center.

stories.headline: Melbourne couple allegedly beat and starved woman enslaved in their home
stories.summary: A Melbourne couple is facing allegations of threatening, coercing, and enslaving an Indonesian woman to provide domestic services in their home.

stories.headline: Middle East crisis live: Iran attacks tanker in Dubai; explosions in Tehran and Jerusalem amid wave of strikes
stories.summary: A fire broke out on a giant tanker in Dubai after an alleged Iranian attack, while explosions were reported in Tehran and Jerusalem amidst a wave of strikes in the Middle East.

stories.headline: Zomi Frankcom’s brother demands audio of deadly Israeli strike but ambassador says ‘it’s in the IDF’s hands’
stories.summary: On the second anniversary of aid worker Zomi Frankcom's killing in Gaza, her family is renewing calls for an open and transparent investigation and demanding audio of the deadly Israeli strike.

stories.headline: Expat influencers sold Dubai to the world and were paid to look the other way. Now the dream is crumbling | Brigid Delaney
stories.summary: An analysis suggests that the glamorous image of Dubai, often promoted by expat influencers, is crumbling, revealing a lonely place built by oppressed people behind the facade.

stories.headline: 13-year-old girl charged with nearly running over Jewish families in antisemitic attack
stories.summary: A 13-year-old girl has been charged with 52 offenses, including allegedly attempting to run over Jewish families in what authorities are describing as an antisemitic attack.

stories.headline: Alleged Cobblebank murder weapon found in father’s shed, court told
stories.summary: During a court hearing, it was revealed that the mother of one of the teenagers accused in the Cobblebank child killing found her son with a machete-style weapon the day after the alleged incident.

stories.headline: South Australia claim back-to-back Sheffield Shield titles
stories.summary: The Redbacks have successfully defeated Victoria in the 2025-26 Sheffield Shield final, securing their second consecutive championship title.

stories.headline: After 16 years and $8 billion, the military's new GPS software still doesn't work
stories.summary: The military's new GPS software, after 16 years and $8 billion in investment, is still not functional, raising concerns about the program's future.

stories.headline: Water utility announces it's ditching fluoride—then reveals it did so years ago: The water utility highlighted unsubstantiated health concerns.
stories.summary: A water utility announced it would stop adding fluoride, only to reveal it had already done so years prior, citing unsubstantiated health concerns.

stories.headline: Judge halts Nexstar/Tegna merger after FCC let firms exceed TV ownership limit: "Defendants must immediately cease" actions to integrate and consolidate the firms.
stories.summary: A judge has halted the Nexstar/Tegna merger, ordering the companies to cease integration efforts after the FCC allowed them to exceed TV ownership limits.

stories.headline: Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones: After operating in secrecy for years, a startup company called R3 Bio, in Richmond, California, suddenly shared details about its work last week—saying it had raised money to create nonsentient monkey
stories.summary: A previously secretive startup, R3 Bio, has revealed its work on creating nonsentient monkey clones, having raised funds for this controversial research.

stories.headline: A woman’s uterus has been kept alive outside the body for the first time: “Think of this as a human body,” says Javier González. In front of me is essentially a metal box on wheels. Standing at around a meter in height, it reminds me of a stainless-steel counter in a restau
stories.summary: For the first time, a woman's uterus has been successfully kept alive outside the body, marking a significant advancement in medical technology.

stories.headline: There are more AI health tools than ever—but how well do they work?: Earlier this month, Microsoft launched Copilot Health, a new space within its Copilot app where users will be able to connect their medical records and ask specific questions about their health. A cou
stories.summary: With a growing number of AI health tools like Microsoft's Copilot Health, questions are arising about their effectiveness and reliability in managing personal medical records.

stories.headline: Meet the Man Making Music With His Brain Implant: Galen Buckwalter says brain-computer interfaces will have to be enjoyable to use if the technology is going to be successful.
stories.summary: Galen Buckwalter is creating music using a brain implant, highlighting the importance of making brain-computer interfaces enjoyable for wider adoption.

stories.headline: 5 Best Video Doorbell Cameras (2026): Smart, Battery, AI, Budget, and Subscription-Free: Never miss a delivery. These WIRED-tested picks will help you keep tabs on your front door from anywhere.
stories.summary: WIRED has compiled a list of the top 5 video doorbell cameras for 2026, offering smart, battery-powered, AI-enabled, budget-friendly, and subscription-free options to monitor your front door.

stories.headline: The Best Earplugs for Protecting Your Hearing (2026): Whether you want to sleep through the party or rock out (safely) to your favorite band, these earplugs will help block out the noise.
stories.summary: A guide to the best earplugs for 2026 is available, designed to protect hearing in various situations, from sleeping to attending loud concerts.

stories.headline: As more Americans adopt AI tools, fewer say they can trust the results: AI adoption is rising in the U.S., but trust remains low, with most Americans concerned about transparency, regulation, and the technology’s broader societal impact, according to a new Quinnipiac poll
stories.summary: Despite increasing adoption of AI tools in the U.S., a new Quinnipiac poll indicates that trust in AI remains low, with concerns about transparency and societal impact.

stories.headline: 15% of Americans say they’d be willing to work for an AI boss, according to new poll: According to a Quinnipiac University poll, 15% of Americans say they'd be willing to have a job where their direct supervisor was an AI program that assigned tasks and set schedules.
stories.summary: A Quinnipiac University poll reveals that 15% of Americans would be open to having an AI program as their direct supervisor for tasks and scheduling.

stories.headline: Allbirds is selling for $39 million. It raised nearly 10 times that amount in its IPO.: The collapse of the brand, which was originally venture-backed and that went public in 2021, has been well-documented.
stories.summary: Allbirds, once a venture-backed company that went public in 2021, is now being sold for $39 million, a fraction of its initial IPO valuation.

stories.headline: AI data centres can warm surrounding areas by up to 9.1°C
stories.summary: Data centers that power AI can cause local average temperatures to rise by up to 9.1°C in areas where hundreds of millions of people live.

stories.headline: Food shock is inevitable due to the Iran war – and it could get bad
stories.summary: Even if the Iran conflict were to end now, higher fuel, fertilizer, and pesticide costs will lead to a food shock in the coming months, with no easy solution.

stories.headline: The profound effect the heart-brain connection has on your health: Cognitive decline, mental health and heart disease are all shaped by the deep links between heart and brain – with major implications for diagnoses and treatment
stories.summary: The intricate connection between the heart and brain significantly influences cognitive decline, mental health, and heart disease, with important implications for diagnosis and treatment.

stories.headline: Millions of new solar system objects to be found and 'filmed in technicolor' -- studies predict
stories.summary: New research predicts that a new facility launching later this year will detect millions of new solar system objects.

stories.headline: Molecular link between air pollution and pregnancy risks
stories.summary: A new study has found a connection between exposure to tiny air pollution particles during pregnancy and an increased risk of negative birth outcomes.

stories.headline: Decades-old assumptions about brain plasticity upended
stories.summary: A new study challenges long-held neuroscience beliefs, showing the brain uses distinct transmission sites for different types of plasticity, not a shared one.

stories.headline: STAT+: Why this Stanford psychiatrist thinks diet can influence serious mental health disorders
stories.summary: A Stanford psychiatrist is at the forefront of metabolic psychiatry, exploring the connection between diet and severe mental health conditions like schizophrenia and bipolar disorder.

stories.headline: Lead still raises risk of heart disease, years after exposure, study warns
stories.summary: A JAMA study indicates that lead exposure can elevate blood pressure, harm blood vessel lining, and increase the risk of heart attack years after initial exposure.

stories.headline: STAT+: Early signs of Alzheimer’s often go undetected. These researchers want to change that
stories.summary: Researchers are using AI to analyze brain scan changes, achieving nearly 93 percent accuracy in predicting Alzheimer’s disease in its early stages.

stories.headline: Kuwaiti oil tanker struck off Dubai: Oil pares gains as fire on vessel under control and Asian markets extend sell-off
stories.summary: A Kuwaiti oil tanker was struck off Dubai, causing oil prices to pare gains after an initial surge, as the fire on the vessel was brought under control.

stories.headline: Foreign central banks sell US Treasuries in wake of Iran war: International official holdings at New York Federal Reserve fall to lowest level since 2012
stories.summary: International official holdings of US Treasuries at the New York Federal Reserve have fallen to their lowest level since 2012, as foreign central banks sell off assets following the Iran conflict.

stories.headline: Swiss lawmakers signal compromise on $22bn UBS capital plan: Decision could be published in April with foreign capital requirements component moving to parliament for debate
stories.summary: Swiss lawmakers are signaling a potential compromise on a $22 billion capital plan for UBS, with a decision possibly coming in April and foreign capital requirements debated in parliament.

stories.headline: Iran Strikes Fully Laden Kuwait Oil Tanker in Dubai Port: An Iranian drone hit a fully laden Kuwaiti oil tanker off Dubai early on Tuesday in one of the most significant attacks on a vessel in a month of war, pushing crude prices higher and heightening tensi
stories.summary: An Iranian drone struck a fully laden Kuwaiti oil tanker in Dubai, escalating tensions and causing crude prices to rise significantly.

stories.headline: Goldman Still Sees Gold at $5,400 By Year-End Despite Downturn: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. retained its bullish view on gold despite the recent selloff, forecasting renewed gains by the end of 2026.
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs maintains a bullish outlook on gold, predicting renewed gains to $5,400 by the end of 2026, despite a recent selloff.

stories.headline: UK Economy Expanded 0.1% in the Fourth Quarter:
stories.summary: The UK economy experienced a modest expansion of 0.1% in the fourth quarter of the year.

stories.headline: How high could global inflation go?: With luck, the Iran war won’t cause a recession. But the surge in energy prices will push up the cost of living
stories.summary: The Iran war is unlikely to cause a recession, but the resulting surge in energy prices is expected to significantly increase the cost of living globally, pushing up inflation.

stories.headline: How Iran is making a mint from Donald Trump’s war: China is helping the Revolutionary Guards profit from Iranian crude
stories.summary: China is reportedly assisting Iran's Revolutionary Guards in profiting from Iranian crude oil amid the conflict, enabling Iran to benefit economically from the ongoing situation.

stories.headline: The decline and fall of the Roman currency empire: The fate of ancient coins offers uncomfortable lessons about dollar dominance
stories.summary: The historical decline of the Roman currency empire provides cautionary insights into the potential vulnerabilities and future of the dollar's global dominance.

stories.headline:
US Escalation Is the Most Likely Scenario in Iran:

If Donald Trump walks away from the war with Iran now, the threat to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz will remain, risk premia on oil prices will stay permanently higher, and Trump’s own popularity
stories.summary: If Donald Trump disengages from the war with Iran, shipping threats in the Strait of Hormuz will persist, keeping oil price risk premia high and potentially impacting his popularity.

stories.headline:
America Should Beware of Economic Hubris:

Even if the US economy continues outperforming its peers, it will not necessarily remain insulated from the Iran war’s adverse spillovers. Already, higher energy and borrowing costs are exacerbatin
stories.summary: Despite its strong performance, the US economy is not immune to the negative economic ripple effects of the Iran war, including rising energy and borrowing costs.

stories.headline:
The Sad Demise of China’s Economic Debate:

China has never been an open society where ideas can be debated freely, save for one area: economic policy. But such engagement is no longer possible, reflected in the demise of the China Developme
stories.summary: China's once-open discourse on economic policy has diminished, as evidenced by the decline of platforms like the China Development Forum, signaling a broader restriction on free debate.

stories.headline:
Grade Caps are Not a Good Solution to Grade Inflation:

It’s well known that grade inflation has “degraded” the informational content of grades at many colleges. At Harvard, two-thirds of all undergraduate grades are now A’s—up f
stories.summary: Grade caps are not an effective solution to grade inflation, which has significantly devalued the informational content of academic grades, with Harvard seeing two-thirds of undergraduate grades as A's.

stories.headline:
Do Parents Propagate Inequality Among Children?:

The subtitle of the piece is “Evidence From Chinese and Swedish Twins.”  Abstract: Economists have long studied how parental behavior shapes within-family inequality, yet empirical find
stories.summary: Economists are examining whether parental behavior contributes to inequality among children, drawing evidence from studies on Chinese and Swedish twins.

stories.headline:
*The AI Doc*:

The subtitle of the movie is Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist, and here is the trailer. Overall this film was better and smarter than I was expecting.  Intelligent people were allowed to speak, an
stories.summary: The film "The AI Doc: Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist" is lauded as a surprisingly intelligent and well-made documentary, allowing various experts to discuss AI's implications.
